panels.title: Node exporter up
panels.description: 1 means Prometheus can scrape the host exporter.

panels.title: CPU busy
panels.description: Average CPU busy percentage.

panels.title: Memory used
panels.description: Memory used from MemAvailable/MemTotal.

panels.title: Max temperature
panels.description: Max host temperature from hwmon/thermal-zone plus Pi vcgencmd where available.

panels.title: Temperatures
panels.description: Sensor panels show only sensors the host actually exposes. Missing sensors are not treated as healthy.

panels.title: Disk health status
panels.description: Disk health: 1 pass, 0 fail, -1 unknown. Unknown is honest for unsupported Pi/USB/microSD paths.

panels.title: Pi throttling / undervoltage
panels.description: Best-effort Pi early-warning metrics; absence means unavailable/not configured, not healthy.

panels.title: Selected host logs
panels.description: Selected host logs from Alloy/Loki. Prefer the Correlation panels below for triage workflows.

panels.title: Uptime
panels.description: Host uptime from node_boot_time_seconds.

panels.title: Disk temperatures
panels.description: Disk temperatures appear only when SMART/NVMe exposes them.

options.content:
### Host correlation triage
Use the same `$host` and time range across these panels. Start with CPU/load/memory/temperature above, then compare log volume and warning/error spikes below. If a spike lines up, open the recent logs panel or Explore with the same labels/time range.

panels.title: Correlation: log volume by host/job
panels.description: Log line volume from Alloy/Loki. Compare spikes with CPU, memory, disk I/O, temperature, and network panels above.

panels.title: Correlation: warning/error log volume
panels.description: Warning/error-like log line volume. This is intentionally broad for triage, not an alert source of truth.

panels.title: Correlation: service scrape availability
panels.description: Shows whether the metric/log collection path itself was up while investigating a host issue.

panels.title: Correlation: recent warning/error logs
panels.description: Recent warning/error-like log lines for the selected host/time range.

panels.title: Correlation: recent selected host logs
panels.description: Raw recent logs for the selected host/time range. Keep labels visible so host/job/container context is available.

panels.title: Storage SMART health
panels.description: SMART health from the lightweight storage monitor. 1=passed/ok; anything else needs host-level smartctl follow-up.

panels.title: Storage disk temperature
panels.description: SMART disk temperatures. Alert threshold is 55C sustained for 15 minutes.

panels.title: Storage risk counters
panels.description: Counters to watch for disk degradation. Historical reallocations are less urgent than increases, pending sectors, or uncorrectable errors.

panels.title: Storage filesystem usage
panels.description: Filesystem usage from the custom storage monitor. Filters pseudo/container filesystems before export.

panels.title: NVMe wear used
panels.description: NVMe percentage-used metric where the disk exposes it.

panels.title: Windows exporter up
panels.description: 1 means Prometheus can scrape windows_exporter. Laptop sleep/offline is normal; alerts only fire after days.

panels.title: Windows CPU busy
panels.description: Low-overhead CPU view from windows_exporter cpu collector.

panels.title: Windows memory used
panels.description: Physical memory used on Windows laptops.

panels.title: Windows C: free
panels.description: C: drive free percentage; alert threshold is below 10% for 30 minutes.

panels.title: Windows scrape duration
panels.description: Keeps overhead visible; homelap is expected to remain well below 1s per 5-minute scrape.

panels.title: Windows ACPI thermal zone
panels.description: Observational Windows ACPI thermal-zone value from textfile collector. May not equal CPU package temperature; graph before alerting.

panels.title: Windows thermal probe success
panels.description: 1 means the PowerShell textfile collector successfully read at least one ACPI thermal zone.

panels.title: Windows thermal trend
panels.description: Trend the ACPI thermal-zone value before deciding whether it is alert-worthy.

list.name: host
list.description: Canonical host selector. Includes Linux node_exporter hosts and Windows laptop windows_exporter targets via monitored_host labels.

title: Host Observability
description: Correlates home-network host metrics, sensor telemetry, disk health, and Loki logs by host/time range.
